article_meta.title: — News —Study Shows Pandemic Saw Rise In Firearm Injuries Among KidsBy Charlotte Pence Bond•Dec 20, 2022 DailyWire.com•F

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language, avoids the article’s emotive framing, and attributes interpretations to researchers. It does not reproduce loaded terms like “disastrous” or “spike” as objective fact. One minor issue: a key fact says researchers “attribute” causes rather than “suggest/might,” slightly overstating certainty.

custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: One key fact states researchers “attribute” increases to stress/policies; the article says they “might have played a role.”
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as facts; evaluative points are attributed.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No amplified or emotive language; avoids the source’s ‘disastrous’ framing.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unspoken intentions or mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focuses on reported studies and data; no opposing perspective was present to skew.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes the main studies, CDC/KFF data, and media attributions present in the article.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects the article’s negative findings without overemphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats academic and media sources neutrally without undue deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source; avoids charged framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents the studies and cited outlets/authors proportionally.
